operating_state: Total revenue declined 9% to $19.34 billion driven by automotive sales decreasing 21% from lower Model 3/Y deliveries due to simultaneous factory changeovers to New Model Y production. Energy generation and storage revenue surged 67% from increased Megapack and Powerwall deployments while services revenue grew 15% from higher Supercharging, insurance, and used vehicle sales. Automotive regulatory credits increased 35% as competitors scaled back electric vehicle plans.
cost_structure: Total gross margin compressed to 16.3% from 17.4% as automotive gross margin fell to 16.2% from 18.5% due to lower average selling prices from sales mix and customer incentives, partially offset by reduced raw material costs and favorable foreign exchange impacts. Energy storage gross margin expanded to 28.8% from 24.6% driven by higher-margin product mix and lower per-unit costs. Research and development expenses increased 22% primarily from AI program investments while selling expenses decreased 9% from reduced employee costs.
material_events: Tesla executed industry-first simultaneous production line changeovers across all factories for New Model Y launch, resulting in several weeks of lost production but demonstrating operational capabilities. The company received proceeds from directors in shareholder settlement arrangements. Bitcoin holdings generated $125 million mark-to-market loss compared to $335 million gain in prior year.
macro_exposures: Evolving trade policy creates uncertainty with higher tariffs potentially impacting global supply chain costs and component availability, with relatively larger impact expected on energy business versus automotive. Rising interest rates from central bank responses to inflation continue affecting vehicle financing affordability and customer demand. Foreign currency fluctuations on significant intercompany balances create volatility as the company does not typically hedge foreign exchange risk.
forward_outlook: Management expects capital expenditures to exceed $10 billion in 2025 for AI infrastructure, factory expansion, and new product development, though trade policy changes may necessitate project timeline adjustments. The company targets profitable growth through leveraging existing capacity for more affordable products, advancing FSD capabilities including Robotaxi development, and expanding energy storage production.
uncertainty_profile: Trade policy scope and application remain uncertain with potential for retaliatory tariffs and export controls affecting production costs and market access. Battery cell supply adequacy for energy storage products faces challenges from potential import tariff increases on battery components. Manufacturing ramp uncertainties persist across concurrent international projects with ambitious technological targets.
